content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 3 correctness issues in the milestone lifecycle work: real controller failures never enter `failed`, pause transitions can persist contradictory `active_bead`/metadata, and duration metadata undercounts resumed executions.

## Amendments

1. `src/cli/run.rs:180-187` and `src/cli/run.rs:329-360` never map a terminal Ralph run failure into `MilestoneStatus::Failed`; they only finalize the bead as `TaskRunOutcome::Failed`. After that, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:791-796` can only reconcile the milestone back to `paused`/`ready`. In production, the required `running -> failed` lifecycle transition is therefore unreachable and controller failures are mislabeled as resumable pauses.
   Rationale: This misses one of the required lifecycle states in the real execution path and prevents the corresponding journal events from ever being emitted.
2. `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:598-606` does not clear `active_bead` when transitioning to `paused`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:204-221` does not reject that combination. Calling `update_status(..., MilestoneStatus::Paused, ...)` can therefore persist a snapshot that says execution is paused while `active_bead` still names a bead as currently executing. The same path also builds pause-event metadata from the pre-transition snapshot at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1068-1076`, so the journal row can report stale running-state metadata.
   Rationale: Paused is part of the new lifecycle API; leaving running-only fields populated creates contradictory state and incorrect journal data for consumers.
3. `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:499-511` plus `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:554-557` compute `duration_seconds` from only the most recent `running` transition. After any pause/resume cycle, the later `completed`/`failed` event drops all earlier running time and reports just the final segment.
   Rationale: The milestone journal will underreport completion/failure duration for any resumed milestone, which breaks the lifecycle tracking this change is meant to add.
